items.title: She travelled 900 km for an abortion. Years later, Quebec access issues remain urgent
Charge squander prime chance, now face uphill battle to make PWHL post-season
items.summary_auto: The Charge, who have now lost three straight, had the chance to climb into the fourth and final playoff spot in the PWHL standings, but fell 5-3 to the last-place Seattle Torrent on Wednesday. Seattle was playing the second game of a back-to-back set.

items.title: Woman who died in Ontario hospital shouldn’t have been in wait room, needed frequent assessment, inquest told
items.summary_auto: On the day she died of sepsis, Heather Winterstein shouldn't have been placed in the emergency room waiting area at the St. Catharines hospital until she could see a doctor, an emergency medicine expert told a coroner's inquest Wednesday.

items.title: Heather Winterstein shouldn’t have been in wait room, needed frequent checks, medical expert tells inquest
items.summary_auto: On the day she died of sepsis, Heather Winterstein shouldn't have been placed in the emergency room waiting area at the St. Catharines hospital until she could see a doctor, an emergency medicine expert told a coroner's inquest Wednesday.

items.title: Alberta government faces another legal fight to keep supervised consumption sites open
items.summary_auto: Less than a year after a judge ruled on a similar case in Red Deer, the provincial government is facing an injunction seeking to stop supervised consumption services from closing in Calgary and Lethbridge.

items.title: As another firefighting season nears, northern Manitoba mayors hope for more equipment
items.summary_auto: Preparations for the upcoming wildfire season are underway as the snow melts in Manitoba, but some mayors in the province's north say they need more help.

items.title: Iran clamping down in Straits of Hormuz in tenuous ceasefire
items.summary_auto: The Middle East ceasefire appeared shaky less than two days after it was announced by U.S. President Donald Trump, as Israel bombed more targets in Lebanon on Thursday and Iran showed little sign of relinquishing its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Why human eyes are better at observing the moon than cameras
items.summary_auto: Human eyes are a wonder of nature. While we've invented the camera to work like our eyes, it's not as good as the real thing, something that was highlighted during Artemis II's lunar flyby.

items.title: Liberals descend on Montreal for convention on the cusp of a majority government
items.summary_auto: Liberals are gathering in Montreal for the next few days, buzzing on a fifth floor crossing and with a sought-after majority government in sight.

items.title: Air Canada is testing a new program to resolve customer complaints. Will it work?
items.summary_auto: Amid a backlog of complaints in the Canadian Transport Agency's complaints system, Air Canada is piloting a new program that will outsource complaints to a third-party arbitrator. Experts say it's good Air Canada is trying to find a solution, but oversight will be needed to make sure the new system doesn't favour companies over consumers.

items.title: You can now get a prescription for nature access if you live in Hamilton, here's how
items.summary_auto: The PaRx program, an initiative that allows healthcare providers to prescribe nature passes to patients, is now available for Hamilton Conservation Authority (HCA) sites.

items.title: Trump and Iran remain far apart on peace terms. Here are the biggest gaps
items.summary_auto: Despite U.S. President Donald Trump's sunny assessment of the prospects for a long-term peace agreement with Iran, there are fundamental differences in the visions the two sides have put forward for a deal.

items.title: Volkswagen Beetle removed from rock face in Squamish, B.C.
items.summary_auto: A group claiming to represent a group of anonymous University of B.C. engineering students took credit for suspending the car last week, as part of a long-running tradition of pranks.

items.title: Blue Jays come back for 4-3 win over Dodgers to end 6-game losing streak
items.summary_auto: With his team's offence mired in a rut, Blue Jays manager John Schneider was hoping a big swing or two would help kickstart his sputtering club.

items.title: For more than 4 decades, a mega-music fan recorded over 10,000 concerts. Now many are online
items.summary_auto: Although Aadam Jacobs concert-recording career has come to an end, his love of music and recordings continues to live online, acting as a valuable entity to the music community.

items.title: Blue Jays players hit by nasty bug as they struggle through early season
items.summary_auto: A mix of illness and injuries have hit the Blue Jays hard as the defending American League champions have struggled to maintain a winning record in the early days of the new season.

items.title: B.C. woman who was detained in Texas speaks out on harm to children in ICE custody
items.summary_auto: Tania Warner and her daughter Ayla Lucas were released from an immigration facility in Texas on April 2, after being held for nearly three weeks. The woman from Penticton, B.C., says she wants to speak out about her experience in custody.

items.title: Curling and golf’s kinship on display as Rock League meets Masters
items.summary_auto: In Scotland, where both golf and curling originated around the 16th century, the sports go hand in hand — and similarities from strategy to sportsmanship to tradition abound.

items.title: MAGA influencers are fed up with Trump
items.summary_auto: A lot's changed since the 2024 U.S. federal election that saw President Donald Trump return to the White House. CBC's Katie Simpson explains how some of Trump's most vocal supporters — right-wing influencers and public figures — are turning their backs on the president amid his recent moves with the Iran war.

items.title: Survivor of intimate partner violence asks P.E.I. MLAs for better protection for victims
items.summary_auto: Clare’s Law allows police officers to provide an individual’s history of abusive behaviour to those who may be at risk. It was named after Clare Wood, a 36-year-old woman in the U.K. who was murdered by a former domestic partner who police knew to be dangerous.

items.title: Former Ontario student pleads guilty in NYC Jewish centre terrorism plot
items.summary_auto: An ISIS supporter who came to Canada on a study permit and planned a mass shooting targeting Jews in New York City has pleaded guilty to a terrorism charge in a U.S. court.

items.title: On-ice expectations were high for the Vancouver Goldeneyes. What went wrong?
items.summary_auto: The Goldeneyes capped off a five-game road trip with a 5-1 loss to Boston on Tuesday. It was the team's fourth loss in five games.

items.title: Class-action lawsuit targets Quebec company accused of producing bogus maple syrup
items.summary_auto: A company accused of producing bogus maple syrup is now the target of a class-action lawsuit request that has been filed in Quebec Superior Court.

items.title: Stars and producers of The Testaments feel the show's message is relevant 'because of Margaret's vision'
items.summary_auto: The Testaments, an adaptation of Margaret Atwood's book, is releasing its first three episodes on Disney+ today and looking to expand on the chillingly regressive future predicted in The Handmaid's Tale. Its stars and creators talk to CBC News about the message and intent of the series.

items.title: Dean Penney's mother pointed to another suspect 1 day after Jennifer Hillier-Penney disappeared
items.summary_auto: Ruby Penney went to the RCMP on Dec. 2, 2016, and told them about a strange phone call she overheard between Jennifer Hillier-Penney and her cousin Derick Hillier. Ruby Penney is not alive to testify, and Derick Hillier is not alive to defend himself.

items.title: Deputy minister broke rules by hiring unqualified acquaintance: ethics watchdog
items.summary_auto: Deputy minister of national defence Christiane Fox broke conflict of interest rules by hiring an old acquaintance when she was the deputy immigration minister, the federal ethics watchdog has found.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts prepare for experience of 'riding a fireball through the atmosphere' to get home
items.summary_auto: The Artemis II crew woke to the sounds of Queen and David Bowie's classic Under Pressure as they continued their journey through space Wednesday. Now preparing for Friday's re-entry, the astronauts also reflected on watching the eclipse of the Earth, the camaraderie of the mission and humanity's purpose on Earth.

items.title: Despite ceasefire with Iran, Israel's aggressive new attacks on Beirut signal no relief for Lebanon
items.summary_auto: The announcement of a ceasefire between Iran and the United States was met in Lebanon with the war’s bloodiest day, as Israel launched a massive air campaign that its media termed "Operation Eternal Darkness." The Israeli strikes killed and wounded hundreds, as Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu signalled his intent to pursue Hezbollah regardless of international pressure.

items.title: Judge gives 15-year sentence to woman who admitted selling Friends star Matthew Perry ketamine
items.summary_auto: A federal judge on Wednesday handed down a sentence of 15 years in prison to a woman who pleaded guilty to selling Friends star Matthew Perry the ketamine that killed him in 2023.
